feedbackLoop.expectedFailingSignal: The test fails with the user-reported behavior
feedbackLoop.actualFailingSignal: Observed failure output from the current repo
feedbackLoop.symptomMatchEvidence: Failure output matches the reported symptom, not a nearby unrelated failure
feedbackLoop.sharpeningPlan: Narrow setup or assertions if the loop becomes slow or broad

patternAnalysis.notes: Pattern evidence belongs in planning/tasks.md#Root Cause Contract

boundaryProbes.inputObserved: Request payload matches the reproduced failure
boundaryProbes.configEnvObserved: Relevant env/config values recorded in planning/tasks.md#Root Cause Contract

backwardTrace.immediateFailureSite: file:line or operation where the symptom appears
backwardTrace.directCaller: caller that passed the bad value or state
backwardTrace.badValueOrigin: where the invalid data/state first appears
backwardTrace.originalTrigger: user action, command, event, config, or dependency response that starts the chain
backwardTrace.symptomSiteFixRejectedBecause: Guarding only the failure site would leave the bad upstream contract intact

rootCauseProof.symptomSite: User-visible failure, command, UI state, log, or bad artifact
rootCauseProof.firstBadState: First field, artifact, cache entry, queue message, config, response, or memory object that becomes invalid
firstBadStateEvidence: Boundary probe or trace evidence from planning/tasks.md#Root Cause Contract
rootCauseProof.violatedContract: Schema, invariant, API contract, state transition, trust boundary, or timing condition violated
rootCauseProof.originalTrigger: User action, command, event, recent diff, config change, or external response that created the first bad state

counterfactualProof.method: Restore the contract, replace input, roll back diff, fix config, or assert the boundary
counterfactualProof.expectedObservation: The first bad state no longer forms and the symptom disappears
counterfactualProof.actualObservation: Observed evidence from the current repo

rootCauseProof.escapeReason: Why tests, types, validators, monitoring, review, or artifact gates missed it

differencesFound: Working path validates input before persistence

diagnosticInstrumentation.questionAnswered: Which boundary first emits the invalid value?
diagnosticInstrumentation.expectedSignal: Probe records invalid value before the failure site
diagnosticInstrumentation.actualSignal: Observed evidence from the current repo
diagnosticInstrumentation.cleanupRequirement: Remove temporary probe or convert it into a durable assertion/log

candidateHypotheses.whyPlausible: Reproduction output points to the affected contract
candidateHypotheses.prediction: The failing signal disappears when that contract is restored

rootCauseHypothesis.falsificationMethod: Command, log probe, assertion, or code-path check
rootCauseHypothesis.expectedObservation: What should be observed if the hypothesis is true
rootCauseHypothesis.actualObservation: Observed evidence from the current repo

evidenceFor: Reproduction output points to the affected code path

correctTestSeam.testSeam: public interface or end-to-end path that reaches the real trigger chain
correctTestSeam.realTriggerChainCoverage: The test enters through the same trigger path as the bug
correctTestSeam.whyShallowTestRejected: A lower-level unit test would not prove the upstream contract

tasks.title: [TEST] Capture the bug as a failing reproduction
acceptance: The target bug is reproduced as a stable failure
The failing loop matches the user-reported symptom
The regression test uses the correct seam for the real trigger chain
The failure output points to the confirmed root-cause path
The reproduction preserves evidence for first bad state and original trigger

notes: Do not repair the symptom site unless Root Cause Contract proves it is the original trigger
Do not change unrelated contracts in this task
